Senior Product Operations Analyst

Northstar Health is hiring a Senior Product Operations Analyst to help product,
design, engineering, customer success, and clinical operations teams make better
decisions as its care coordination platform expands into new markets. This role
turns ambiguous information into clear operating processes, useful reporting,
and practical recommendations. The ideal candidate understands how software
product teams operate and enjoys improving the systems behind their work.

The analyst will partner with product managers on roadmap priorities, customer
feedback, launch timelines, and operational constraints. The work requires
synthesizing support tickets, customer interviews, CRM records, analytics
dashboards, sales feedback, implementation notes, and internal planning
documents. The analyst should identify patterns, surface risks early, and help
teams decide where process, tooling, or communication changes will have the
greatest impact.

Candidates should be able to create adaptable, repeatable processes for roadmap
intake, release readiness, customer feedback triage, and post-launch
measurement. They must be able to document decisions, maintain clear owners and
next steps, and follow through consistently. Strong written communication is
important because recommendations must be concise and useful to stakeholders
who cannot review every raw data source or meeting note.

This role spends meaningful time working with product and operational data.
Candidates do not need to be data scientists, but they should be comfortable
querying or analyzing data, finding inconsistencies, and explaining what the
numbers do and do not prove. SQL experience is strongly preferred. Experience
with a BI tool such as Looker, Tableau, Mode, or Power BI is helpful. Strong
spreadsheet skills are needed for quick analysis, scenario modeling, and
operational tracking. Candidates should be able to combine quantitative
findings with qualitative customer feedback instead of relying on a single
source.

Because the product supports clinical workflows, attention to detail matters.
The analyst will coordinate launches across product, engineering,
customer-facing teams, and operational stakeholders. Responsibilities include
preparing launch checklists, tracking dependencies, confirming enablement
materials, monitoring early adoption, and coordinating follow-up when customers
experience confusion or friction. Experience in healthcare, care delivery,
insurance, another regulated industry, or a compliance-heavy environment is
valuable but not required when the candidate has strong product operations
experience elsewhere.

Strong cross-functional collaboration is required. Candidates should be
comfortable facilitating meetings, asking clarifying questions, resolving
conflicting input, and helping teams leave discussions with clear decisions and
owners. Experience supporting agile product teams, working with Jira or Linear,
maintaining documentation in Confluence or Notion, and coordinating release
planning across multiple teams is preferred.

Candidates should have at least five years of experience in product operations,
business operations, product analytics, program management, consulting, or a
similar role supporting software teams. B2B SaaS experience is preferred,
especially work with enterprise customers or complex implementation cycles.
This is a senior individual-contributor role that requires independent
prioritization, sound judgment, and knowing when to escalate decisions.

Typical work includes building a dashboard to track feature adoption, reviewing
customer feedback for recurring workflow problems, preparing a concise summary
of launch risks, improving the product intake process, and helping a product
manager distinguish a one-off customer request from a broader market pattern.
The analyst may also maintain quarterly planning, roadmap reviews, beta-program
tracking, customer-advisory-board follow-up, and post-launch retrospectives.

The strongest candidates are curious, pragmatic, and clear communicators. They
can move from detailed analysis to an executive summary without losing
important context, make messy information usable, and state when available data
is incomplete or a recommendation depends on judgment.

This is a remote-friendly United States role. Candidates must be available for
core working hours aligned with the Eastern or Central time zones and for
occasional travel two to four times per year.
